dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, the situation has escalated. They got their hands on inferior substitutes while you were out and tried to work with them anyway. Nearly destroyed everything.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} pulled them out before the worst happened. They were furious: not grateful, furious. Said they were close to a breakthrough on the {quest_focus}. {quest_ally} found them trying to trade their last possessions for more materials.
dialogueChunks.outro: If you found the original {quest_item}, bring it now. They're past the point of reason and the next shortcut might be the one that kills them.

playerResponses.text: I have what they need. Let's hope it's enough to stop the spiral.
Maybe the disaster should have been the wake-up call.

dialogueChunks.intro: They tried the substitutes. The result was visible from three settlements away. The workshop is still standing, but barely.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} found them in the wreckage, already mixing another batch with bleeding hands. They weren't even shaken: they were taking notes. {quest_ally} tried to reason with them and they said: 'I'm closer than anyone has ever been to the {quest_focus}. You want me to stop now?' The {enemy_type} camp where {quest_item} was taken is still reachable, but the path is dangerous.
dialogueChunks.outro: Get {quest_item} before they try again with something worse. The next attempt might not leave anything standing.

playerResponses.text: I'll bring the real materials. At least the next attempt won't be suicidal.
They need to be stopped, not supplied.

dialogueChunks.intro: Their family came to see them yesterday. First time in years. They brought a grandchild they've never met.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: They barely looked up. {target_npc} says the family stood there for ten minutes before the tears started. {quest_ally} tried to intervene but they just said: 'After I find the last piece of the {quest_focus}. Then I'll have time.'
dialogueChunks.outro: The final piece was last traced to {location_hint}. Find it. Not for them: for the family that's watching them disappear into the {quest_focus} one day at a time.

playerResponses.text: That family deserves their loved one back. I'll find the keystone.
What if the piece isn't there? Will they finally stop?
Their family needs to accept that they've made their choice.
